name: Astryx
description: A nine-component slice of Meta's Astryx design system (open source, MIT; 160+ components; explicitly guidance-over-enforcement: design opinions live in docs, components render whatever they are passed). This contract converts a subset of that prose guidance into machine-checkable governance. Components and guidance are sourced from the repository's structured *.doc.mjs files at the pinned tag.

alert-dialog.name: AlertDialog
alert-dialog.description: A modal dialog that asks the user to confirm a destructive action. Props-based: title, description, and action/cancel labels are string props (there is no trigger sub-component; visibility is controlled).
alert-dialog.whenToUse: AlertDialog asks the user to confirm a destructive or irreversible action before it happens. Use it for things like deleting content, revoking access, or discarding unsaved changes.
alert-dialog.whenNotToUse: Do not use AlertDialog for non-destructive actions; use a standard Dialog instead.

actionLabel.description: Action button label. Make it specific: 'Delete project' is better than 'OK' or 'Confirm'.

dialog.name: Dialog
dialog.description: A modal window over the page content. Takes arbitrary children; dismissal behavior is controlled by `purpose`.
dialog.whenNotToUse: Do not use Dialog for destructive confirmations; use AlertDialog.

variant.description: Dialog variant: fullscreen expands to fill the viewport.

purpose.description: Controls dismissal: 'required' disables Escape and backdrop click; 'form' disables backdrop click only.

composition.notes: Takes arbitrary children (ReactNode); surfaces express them as child nodes.

button.name: Button
button.description: Triggers an action when clicked. The accessible label is a REQUIRED string prop (rendered as visible text; used as aria-label when isIconOnly).
button.whenToUse: Use for form submissions, confirmations, or any interaction that needs a clear call to action.
button.whenNotToUse: Do not use a button for navigation; if it only takes the user to another page, use a link instead.

label.description: Accessible label. Rendered as visible text by default; used as aria-label when isIconOnly.

variant.description: Reserve primary for the single most important action in the view. Use destructive for irreversible actions.

isIconOnly.description: Icon-only rendering; the label becomes the aria-label.

tooltip.description: Tooltip text; recommended for icon-only buttons.

badge.name: Badge
badge.description: A small status or category label.
badge.whenToUse: Badge states where the user needs to notice or act. Use success/warning/error only for system status that demands attention; use color variants for category tags.
badge.whenNotToUse: Do not apply a success badge to every healthy/active item; if all rows show green badges, none of them communicate anything.

variant.description: Status variants (neutral/info/success/warning/error) for system state; color variants for category tags.

label.description: Badge text. Keep to one or two words.

card.name: Card
card.description: A container for a discrete item that could be reordered or removed independently.
card.whenNotToUse: Do not default to cards for visual grouping; a heading plus spacing often creates hierarchy without chrome.

composition.notes: Takes arbitrary children (ReactNode); surfaces express them as child nodes.

text-input.name: TextInput
text-input.description: A single-line text input. The visible label is a REQUIRED string prop.
text-input.whenToUse: Use for short free-form text. Always provide a visible label; only hide it when surrounding context makes the purpose unambiguous.

label.description: Visible field label (required by the component API).

isLabelHidden.description: Visually hides the label (still announced to screen readers).

dropdown-menu.name: DropdownMenu
dropdown-menu.description: A menu opened from a trigger button. DATA-DRIVEN: menu entries are an `items` array prop, not child components — a deliberate Astryx idiom this contract documents as an array-typed prop.

items.description: Menu entries: [{label, ...}]. Handlers inside items are not surface-expressible; generation carries labels only.

hasChevron.description: Shows a chevron on the trigger.

table.name: Table
table.description: Tabular data. DATA-DRIVEN: rows and columns are array props (`data`, `columns`), not sub-components — the second instance of the Astryx data-props idiom.

composition.notes: Takes arbitrary children (ReactNode); surfaces express them as child nodes.

action.name: Action
action.description: Triggers an operation when activated. Mirrors Astryx's own 'Action' docs category.

overlay.name: Overlay
overlay.description: Renders above the page with its own focus and dismiss semantics. Mirrors Astryx's 'Overlay' docs category.

container.name: Container
container.description: Groups discrete content. Mirrors Astryx's 'Container' docs category.

content.name: Content
content.description: Static content presentation. Mirrors Astryx's 'Content' docs category.

data-input.name: Data Input
data-input.description: Collects user input. Mirrors Astryx's 'Data Input' docs category.

feedback.name: Feedback & Status
feedback.description: Communicates state or draws attention. Mirrors Astryx's 'Feedback & Status' docs category.

table-list.name: Table & List
table-list.description: Tabular and list data presentation. Mirrors Astryx's 'Table & List' docs category.

intents.name: Destructive action
intents.description: The requested UI performs an irreversible or high-consequence operation: deleting content, revoking access, discarding unsaved changes.

rules.rationale: "Don't use AlertDialog for non-destructive actions; use a standard Dialog instead" — and its converse: AlertDialog "asks the user to confirm a destructive or irreversible action before it happens". A destructive flow must use the confirmation dialog, not the dismissible one — Astryx AlertDialog docs, v0.1.2

title, description, and actionLabel are required by the component API (aria-labelledby / aria-describedby wiring), and "describe what will happen in the description so the user knows the consequences before confirming". The schema does not check prop presence; this rule does — Astryx AlertDialog docs, v0.1.2

"Make the action button label specific: 'Delete project' is better than 'OK' or 'Confirm'". The forbidden values are the generic labels the guidance names (plus their nearest kin) — Astryx AlertDialog docs, v0.1.2

"Always provide a label for icon-only buttons so screen readers can announce what the button does" — and label is the component's required accessible name in every mode. The schema does not check prop presence; this rule does — Astryx Button docs, v0.1.2

"Always provide a visible label so users know what the field is for. Only hide the label when surrounding context makes the purpose unambiguous" (and then via isLabelHidden, which keeps it announced — never by omitting it) — Astryx TextInput docs, v0.1.2

A dialog is a single layer of focus; stacking another overlay (dialog, alert dialog, dropdown menu) inside it breaks focus containment and dismissal semantics. Astryx renders whatever it is passed (guidance-over-enforcement); this rule is the enforcement its docs' modality guidance implies.

examples.name: Delete project confirmation
examples.prompt: a screen to delete a project
examples.description: A card presenting the destructive entry point (a labeled destructive button) alongside the AlertDialog carrying its required title, consequence description, and a specific action label — the Astryx props-based idiom (no trigger sub-component).

children.text: Deleting a project removes all of its data permanently.

props.title: Delete this project?
props.description: This action cannot be undone. The project and all its data will be permanently removed.
